'''Kruise Leeming''' (born 7 September 1995) is an [[England national rugby league team|England]] international [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|hk}} for the [[Catalans Dragons]]  in the [[Super League]], on a season-long loan from the [[Wigan Warriors]].

He played for the [[Huddersfield Giants]] and [[Leeds Rhinos]] in the [[Super League]] and on loan from [[Huddersfield Giants|Huddersfield]] in 2016 at [[Oldham RLFC|Oldham]] in the [[Championship (rugby league)|Championship]]. At representative level, he has played for [[England national rugby league team|England]], the [[Combined Nationalities rugby league team|Combined Nations All Stars]], and [[England Knights]].

==Background==
Leeming was born in [[Lobamba]], [[Eswatini]] (then Swaziland), but raised in [[Halifax, West Yorkshire, England]].

Kruise is a graduate of the Giants' academy system.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.giantsrl.com/locker-room/profile/391/kruise-leeming|title=Huddersfield Giants - Rugby Super League}}</ref> and was called up to the [[England Knights]] squad in 2019 along with 3 other Giants players.<ref name="yorkshirepost.co.uk">https://www.yorkshirepost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/huddersfield-giants/huddersfield-giants-quartet-are-selected-for-england-knights-squad-1-9674104 {{Dead link|date=February 2022}}</ref>

==Club career==
===Huddersfield Giants===
A product of [[Huddersfield]]'s academy,<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.examinerlive.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/news/kruise-leeming-jared-simpson-lead-9664099|title=Kruise Leeming and Jared Simpson to lead Huddersfield Giants' Academy charge at Widnes Vikings|date=16 July 2015}}</ref> Leeming made his Super League début against [[Warrington Wolves]] in 2013, and scored with his first touch of the ball.<ref>[http://www.examiner.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/news/kruise-leeming-makes-big-impact-4926353 Kruise Leeming makes big impact in Huddersfield Giants debut]</ref> He went on to make one further appearances that year, and four appearances in 2014. Leeming established himself in the Huddersfield first-team in 2015, playing 15 times including an appearance from the bench in each of Huddersfield's [[Rugby League Super 8s|Super 8s]] fixtures.{{cn|date=October 2022}}

He has become a regular first team player since 2015 and was an integral part of the first team squad.

===Leeds Rhinos===
It was announced in early 2020 that Leeming would join Leeds.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/51591124|title=Kruise Leeming: Leeds Rhinos hooker faces four more months out after knee surgery|work=BBC Sport|accessdate=15 Dec 2020}}</ref>

On 17 October 2020, he played in the [[2020 Challenge Cup Final]] victory for Leeds over Salford at [[Wembley Stadium]].<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/54555957|title=Leeds beat Salford 17-16 after Luke Gale lands late winning drop-goal|work=BBC Sport}}</ref>

In round 8 of the [[2021 Super League season]], Leeming scored two tries for Leeds in a 60-16 victory over Castleford.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/57252489|title= Castleford Tigers 6-60 Leeds Rhinos - 11-try visitors pull off stunning win|work= BBC Sport}}</ref>
In the 2021 [[Magic Weekend]], Leeming scored a try and kicked a drop goal in Leeds 25-24 victory over [[Hull FC]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.skysports.com/watch/video/sports/rugby-league/12399485/leemings-first-ever-drop-goal-seals-leeds-win/|title=Leeming's first-ever drop-goal seals Leeds win|website=www.skysports.com}}</ref>
Leeming played a total of 23 games for Leeds in the [[2021 Super League season]] including the club's 36-8 loss against St Helens in the semi-final.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/live/rugby-league/58522487?page=2|title=Super League semi-final: St Helens beat Leeds to reach Grand Final - as it happened|website=www.bbc.co.uk}}</ref>
In round 17 of the 2022 Betfred Super League season, Leeming scored two tries for Leeds in a 62-16 victory over [[Hull F.C.]]<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/61995699|title=Super League: Hull FC 16-62 Leeds Rhinos - Ash Handley scores five tries in rout|work=BBC Sport |date=2 July 2022 }}</ref>
On 24 September 2022, Leeming played for Leeds in their 24-12 loss to [[St Helens RFC]] in the [[2022 Super League Grand Final]]. Leeming scored a try in the first half of the match.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2022/sep/24/st-helens-leeds-super-league-grand-final-match-report-rugby-league/|title=St Helens sink Leeds to win record fourth consecutive Grand Final|newspaper=The Observer|date=24 September 2022 |last1=Bower |first1=Aaron }}</ref>

===Gold Coast===
In March 2023, Leeming requested an immediate release from his Leeds contract which was granted by the club. On 2 April 2023, it was confirmed that Leeming had signed a one-year deal with the [[Gold Coast Titans|Gold Coast]].<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/65154776|title=Gold Coast Titans confirm deal for Leeds Rhinos hooker|work=BBC Sport |date=2 April 2023 }}</ref>
Leeming became the second African-born man to play in the NRL after [[Jarrod Saffy]].{{fact|date=January 2024}}

===Wigan Warriors===	
In July 2023, many clubs approached Leeming but following conversations with coach Matty Peet, Kruise signed a contract to join Super League side Wigan from 2024 on a four-year deal.<ref name="https://www.bbc.com/sport/rugby-league/66018024">{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.com/sport/rugby-league/66018024|title=Kruise Leeming, Sam Waters & Tiaki Chan: Wigan Warriors sign trio for next season|publisher=BBC Sport|date=26 June 2023|accessdate=26 June 2023}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.nrl.com/news/2023/01/01/2023-nrl-signings-tracker-latest-signings-news-details-contracts-signings-updates/|title=2023 NRL Signings Tracker: Bulldogs snare Sexton; Schuster staying put|website=www.nrl.com|date=5 February 2024 }}</ref>
In round 1 of the 2024 Super League season, Leeming made his club debut for Wigan in their 32-4 victory over Castleford.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://ww.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/68304778/|title=Super League: Castleford Tigers 4-32 Wigan Warriors - Reigning champions begin with hard-earned win|website=www.bbc.co.uk}}{{dead link|date=September 2025|bot=medic}}{{cbignore|bot=medic}}</ref>
On 24 February 2024, Leeming played in Wigan's [[2024 World Club Challenge]] final victory over [[Penrith Panthers|Penrith]] scoring a try during the first half.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/68337685|title=World Club Challenge: Wigan Warriors 16-12 Penrith Panthers - Cherry and Whites crowned world champions for fifth time|website=www.bbc.co.uk}}</ref>
On 8 June, Leeming played in Wigan's [[2024 Challenge Cup final]] victory over Warrington from the interchange bench.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/c2jj1x7m71zo|title=Wigan beat Warrington to win Challenge Cup|website=www.bbc.co.uk}}</ref>
On 12 October, Leeming played in Wigan's 9-2 2024 Super League grand final victory over Hull Kingston Rovers.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/live/2024/oct/12/wigan-v-hull-kr-super-league-grand-final-2024-live-score-updates?page=with:block-670aaacb8f087ed1d5a8a350/|title=Wigan v Hull KR: Super League Grand Final 2024 – as it happened|website=www.theguardian.com}}</ref>
On 9 October 2025, Leeming played in Wigan's 24-6 2025 Super League Grand Final loss against Hull Kingston Rovers.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/cx2jw497jpzo/|title=Hull KR beat Wigan in Grand Final to complete treble|website=www.bbc.co.uk}}</ref>

===Catalans Dragons===	
On 18 January 2026 it was reported that he had signed for Catalans Dragons on a season-long loan for the 2026 season from Wigan.

==International career==
In July 2018 he was selected in the [[England Knights]] Performance squad.<ref name="Ten new additions in England Knights squad">{{cite web|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/ten-new-additions-in-england-knights-squad/|title=Ten new additions in England Knights squad|publisher=loverugbyleague|date=24 July 2018|accessdate=31 October 2019}}</ref> Later that year he was selected for the Knights on their tour of [[Papua New Guinea]]. He played against [[Papua New Guinea national rugby league team|Papua New Guinea]] at the Lae Football Stadium.<ref name="Papua New Guinea vs England Knights">{{cite web|url=https://wwos.nine.com.au/nrl/live-scores/papua-new-guinea-v-england-knights-international-2018/718006#lineup|title=Papua New Guinea vs England Knights|publisher=WWOS|date=27 October 2018|accessdate=30 October 2019}}</ref>

Kruise was called up to the [[England Knights]] squad in 2019 and selected for the test against [[Jamaica national rugby league team|Jamaica]] at [[Headingley Rugby Stadium]].<ref name="England Knights vs Jamaica">{{cite web|url=https://wwos.nine.com.au/nrl/live-scores/england-knights-v-jamaica-international-2019/719012#lineup|title=England Knights vs Jamaica|publisher=WWOS|date=20 October 2019|accessdate=30 October 2019}}</ref>

In [[International rugby league in 2021#June|June 2021]] he played for the [[Combined Nationalities rugby league team|Combined Nations All Stars]] in their 26-24 victory over [[England national rugby league team|England]] at [[Halliwell Jones Stadium|Warrington]], as part of England’s preparation for the ultimately postponed [[2021 Rugby League World Cup]].<ref name="England 18-4 Combined Nations All Stars: Shaun Wane's men secure three-try win">{{cite web|url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/england-vs-all-stars/teams/55444|title=England 18-4 Combined Nations All Stars: Shaun Wane's men secure three-try win|publisher=Sky Sports|date=18 June 2021|accessdate=26 June 2023}}</ref> A year later, Leeming featured again as the All-Stars were defeated 18-4 as part of England's preparations for the postponed tournament.<ref name="England Overcome All-Stars Test">{{cite web|url=https://www.rugby-league.com/article/60320/england-overcome-all-stars-test|title=England Overcome All-Stars Test|publisher=Rugby Football League|date=18 June 2022|accessdate=26 June 2023}}</ref>
